text: [4.1 Authorization Policy] 4.1 Authorization Policy • Access must follow the Principle of Least Privilege (PoLP). • Authorization must use Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). • Critical system access must require manager approval. • Access review must occur at least quarterly. • Privileged accounts must be monitored and reviewed monthly.

[4.2 Authentication Policy] 4.2 Authentication Policy • MFA is mandatory for all users accessing corporate systems. • Passwords must follow strong password requirements (length ≥ 12, complexity enforced). • Service accounts must use key-based or certificate-based authentication where possible. • Default credentials must be removed or changed before deployment. • SSO must be used where supported.

[4.3 Logging & Monitoring Policy] 4.3 Logging & Monitoring Policy • All critical systems must log: o authentication attempts o access control changes o configuration or system changes o security events & anomalies • Logs must be: o sent to a central SIEM o retained for a minimum of 1 year o reviewed weekly by the security operations team • Time synchronization (NTP) must be enforced across all systems.

[4.4 Certification & Compliance Policy] 4.4 Certification & Compliance Policy • Relevant systems must comply with applicable industry standards (ISO, SOC, etc.). • Annual internal audits must evaluate implementation of controls. • Corrective actions must be documented and resolved within 90 days. • Third-party services must undergo compliance verification (SOC 2, ISO 27001, etc.).

[4.5 Application Patching Policy] 4.5 Application Patching Policy • All systems must follow a formal patch management lifecycle. • Patch timelines: o Critical patches: within 48 hours o High patches: within 7 days o Medium patches: within 30 days o Low patches: within 90 days • Unsupported or legacy systems must have compensating controls documented.

[4.6 System Hardening Policy] 4.6 System Hardening Policy • Systems must follow secure configuration baselines (CIS, benchmarks, vendor hardening guides). • Unnecessary services, ports, and applications must be disabled or removed. • Encryption (TLS 1.2+) must be enforced for data in transit. • Administrative interfaces must be segregated and restricted. • Hardening guidelines must be reviewed annually.

[4.7 Session Management Policy] 4.7 Session Management Policy • Sessions must timeout after: o 15 minutes of inactivity for users o 5 minutes for privileged users • Session tokens must be: o encrypted o protected against reuse o invalidated immediately on logout or account lock • Applications must prevent: o simultaneous logins (where possible) o predictable session IDs
